item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 59 replies — 236.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-06-21 21:05 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

This whole setup seems bizarre to me, almost like it was designed to hoodwink a bunch of midwits and retards who don't know that business to business API service providers have existed for like 50 years now and none of them have required some stupid fake token to make the service possible.

item.title: Engineer here. Why cant I just find a job were they pay attention to wether I finish the tasks on time(and do a high qua… — 39 replies — 156.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Engineer here. Why cant I just find a job were they pay attention to wether I finish the tasks on time(and do a high qua…

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-21 21:15 UTC

OP

Engineer here. Why cant I just find a job were they pay attention to wether I finish the tasks on time(and do a high quality job of course)? Why do I always have to jump through mental hoops for my non engineering managers.


I cant tell u how many times I have worked 80 hours weeks, got something to work that was borderline impossible to work and made the company money just to get bersted for the most trivial shit like a ossciaonal typo in an email or me sending 2-3 short teams messages in a row instead of one streamlined message. Its not like this is an email to our clients, its a fucking teams message between coworkers. I cant fucking do this anymore, I never had any problem with my more senior engineers or coworkers in general but there is always a MBA asshole manager that does this shit to me. Its been going on for 10 fucking years now. The jobs that had cool managers went under. Is this what it takes to run a successful comapny? Tell yoyr engineers to stop trying to do good technical work and focus on superficial appearances? If i open up my own comapny one say will I turn into what I hate? Is this simply the natrual conclusion to white collar work?

Early replies (first 3)

62393655
>>62393647
You're trying to do something you've never been asked to. Chill the fuck out bro.

62393660
>>62393647
Engineers are adult losers.

It's why so many giga corps care so much about the vibes in their office, they would rather get rid of a 10x worker who brings down the morale of a hundred other employees than deal with it. All you can do is your best to make it a good work environment for yourself and teammates OP and leave for greener pastures if management doesnt share your style.

62393725
>>62393704
Office staff sending me time-sensitive messages about patients instead of coming and talking to me like they should in person because I try to not check Teams. That and the fact I have to be on Tiger and check my personal cell phone regularly for incoming calls and texts.

Attending docs sending me messages asking me to do their work for them and see patients in their schedule so that they can come in late and leave early to drive to the city to fuck divorced men to get back as their ex-husbands.

It's not enough that I see the most number of patients in the office alongside one other guy. I have to do the tasks of others and make the phone calls that the nurses should be making because they're women and they don't work. All of this on top of listening to fat fucks all day not be able to answer yes or no questions. I ask someone about chest pain and they start telling me about how they have chronic knee pain after bumping into a cabinet at the age of 12. And somehow I have to cram all of this into an impossibly small window so that I can get to the next two patients waiting to see me.

62393729
>>62393710
I didnt except any recognition. Instead of telling them they give me a non realistic deadline, I did what needed to be done. It wasnt 80 exactly but I worked well over 60 hours for over 3 months in a row this year and I got screamed over sending 2 short teams messages instead of 1 slightly longer sentance

It is like being a nerd, dork or geek as a kid but now you’re an adult.
Does it matter if the dorky kid has rich parents? No, still a dork.

>>62393681

Me
>"hey so and so resulted in this and that..."
>"yadda yardage I forgot to mention this.."


My boss
>"you are waisting my time bombarding me with messages, work on ur communcation skills"


Of course I stopped casually messaging him but I still message everyone else like that and no one has a problem cuz no one's time is so precious that they need the 1.5 extra seconds they they spend reading my other message. Of course, I treid to do everything perfect cuz I couldn't find another job and last week I had a typo so now i might get fired for it and sadly I am not overexagurating beacuse i keep getting asscued of having bad communcatik skills and he acts like this is a hudge problem that's dragging through company down.

item.title: should I open a Roth IRA or just keep buying voo with my personal brokerage account? — 15 replies — 27.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
should I open a Roth IRA or just keep buying voo with my personal brokerage account?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-21 20:46 UTC

OP

should I open a Roth IRA or just keep buying voo with my personal brokerage account?

Early replies (first 3)

62392790
>>62392778
You should absolutely use some kind of retirement account to avoid the immediate tax burden. I’m personally a big fan of Roths as I believe US taxes as a percentage of income are the lowest they’re ever gonna get and locking in some post-tax earnings at the current heavily discounted rate is a no brainer.

62392806
>>62392790
Just wait until you're 60 for your life to begin lmao. Never mind that the brain starts degrading at 50. Put it all in a brokerage, that way you can at least realize the gains in your prime years if you want to.

62392927
>>62392790
Roth doesn't avoid the immediate tax burden.
Brokerage accounts are subject to capital gains taxes, but the lowest tax bracket for capital gains in the US is 0% tax up to $98k per year (which is adjusted for inflation each year). And unlike with the Roth, there are no restrictions on withdrawing money from a brokerage account.
>inb4 muh SEPP 72(t)
Will pay your monthly bills, but won't help you with a vacation or a new car or the down payment on a house. And that brokerage account will also pay your monthly bills tax-free as long as you keep it under $98k/yr.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62392931
>>62392927
Also that $98k is gains, if you sell a stock that has tripled in value since you bought it, your gain is only 75% of the selling price, so you could withdraw $122k/year without paying taxes.

62392964
>>62392778
401k Advisor Anon here-
Great question! It depends on how much money you are planning to invest and what your time horizon is.
The annual contribution limit for a Roth IRA in 2026 is $7500. Anything you plan to invest this year beyond that amount will have to be in your personal brokerage account.
If you don’t think you’ll need to withdraw funds before retirement, a Roth IRA is the way to go. However, you can tap into a Roth IRA early if necessary. The principal (which is a post tax contribution) can be withdrawn at anytime without tax or penalty. But the real benefit comes if you’re willing to wait. As long as the account is held for at least 5 years, and withdrawn after age 59 1/2, then the capital gains are also tax exempt. If you’re in your 20s or 30s now, this is a monumental advantage. 30-40 years of gains in the market can be exponential.

62393013
>>62392964
>ChatGPT enters the thread

62393017
>>62392790
Correct.
>>62392806
Roth IRA contribution max is only $7500 a year. If that's all you have to invest you were never going to make it anyways. Max out your Roth and invest the rest in a brokerage account.

62393084
>>62393017
>If that's all you have to invest you were never going to make it anyways
I take it you don't know about Peter Thiel's $28B Roth account?

62393169
>>62393013

62393176
>>62392778

62393182
>>62392778

62393354
>>62392778
Why is it so hard for women to look and dress like this 24/7?

62393852
>>62392927
True, but that bracket is for marred filinb together. You start paying capital gains after ~50k single or married filing separately.

>>62392778
The only real opportunity cost of roth ira contributions is the "earnings" (amount above contribute basis and seasoned conversions) are locked until ~60 and it's harder to get leverage. You might even qualify for a tax deduction on traditional contributions if you're in the right bracket.

62393878
>>62392778
If you sincerely believe you will get rich before old age (before 45), then I honestly believe a Roth IRA is pointless. Though if you end up waging and reach 45, then I think you screwed up not using it since your fate is likely going to result in you waging at 60 still.

t. Regret setting up a Roth IRA, my money is now trapped there unless I find some loophole that lets me pull it out. The irony is that it is less than $1000, not even 1% of my networth and yet I regret it. I am not rich but I'm confident I will be.

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Okay guys as we wait for the third topic, let’s go ahead and get started

First off, why should we care about photonics at this point in time? Well while the field itself might sound like sci-fi to the layman (insert schizo theories on light healing, lightsabers or consciousness transfer) we want to focus on the more grounded reality of what we’re dealing with today. AI.

We all know the Jews have massive amounts of data. They have it. How they collected is not relevant nor is the why. They have it. The main reason everyone is talking about photonics right now is because they are struggling to move the data from point A to B

Copper is what has generally handled this role, but they consume significant power, generate heat, and become less efficient at the massive scales Jews are targeting. Copper probably won’t just completely exit the industry however there will most certainly be a reduction.

Okay. Let’s stop here and take a breather. Now let us ask ourselves. What can photonics solve here, that would also make us some money?

>'hardware lottery... describes when a research idea wins because it is suited to the available software and hardware and not because the idea is superior to alternative research directions." - Sara Hooker, The Hardware Lottery, Researcher at Google

39$/hour as a masonry laborer.

I started at 25$/hr in the company’s office, where I used my tech skills to build website stuff, learned and ran Google Ads and got some work for the company, which progressively rewarded me with raises.

I ran out of work at some point due to it being a small company so I was put in the field as a laborer. Now I do grunt work but get paid the same.
